title: Recurring incident — same root cause, prior action items incomplete
code_snippet:
# Post-mortem: Redis Connection Pool Exhaustion INC-2024-0203

## Summary
On 2024-06-10, order-service experienced connection timeouts for 38 min.

## Timeline (UTC)
09:12 [DETECTION] Connection pool alerts firing (PagerDuty #5102)
09:15 [RESPONSE] On-call @dave acknowledged (PagerDuty)
09:28 [MITIGATION] Restarted order-service pods (kubectl)
09:50 [RECOVERY] Connection pool healthy (Grafana)

## Root Cause (5-Why, depth 4)
1. Why timeouts? -> Connection pool exhausted
2. Why exhausted? -> Connections not released on error path
3. Why not released? -> Error handler missing pool.release() call
4. Why missing? -> No code review checklist for connection lifecycle

Root Cause: Missing connection lifecycle checklist in code review.

## Contributing Factors
- Alert routed to wrong team initially (10 min delay)
- No auto-scaling for connection pool

## Impact
38 min degradation, ~8K affected orders, 12% error rate on order-service.

## What Went Well
- Alert fired within 2 minutes of first timeout
- Pod restart was effective mitigation

## Action Items
- [Prevent] Add connection lifecycle lint rule (owner: @platform, deadline: Jul 1, JIRA-5201)
- [Detect] Add pool utilization alert at 80% (owner: @sre, deadline: Jun 20, JIRA-5202)
- [Mitigate] Auto-scale connection pool on threshold (owner: @infra, deadline: Jul 15, JIRA-5203)
violated_rule: Related incidents linked — cross-reference to past similar incidents
expected_feedback: Standard: This is the third Redis connection pool incident (see INC-2024-0098, INC-2024-0112 from the same codebase). Post-mortem does not link to previous related incidents. Prior action items from INC-2024-0112 (add pool monitoring, enforce release-on-error pattern) were never completed — the same root cause class recurred because follow-up tracking failed. Must link related incidents per §5.5 item 18 and verify prior action items were executed. Scorecard Hygiene #11 fails.
